jokes.text: Tomorrow is Father's Day, which makes the vigil tonight for Father's Day. Joy to the world, a father was born.
jokes.setup: Homer treats Father's Day like Christmas with religious reverence
jokes.punchline: Creating a fake 'Father's Day vigil' and adapting Christmas carols
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer absurdist logic - treating Father's Day with Christmas-level reverence is perfectly in character and original

jokes.text: No vigil Father's Day? This means that there no... Paternal Christmas?
jokes.setup: Homer's disappointment about no Father's Day vigil
jokes.punchline: Homer inventing 'Paternal Christmas' as if it's a real thing he's missing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the Father's Day absurdity with a made-up holiday

jokes.text: Is there a Rabbit the SuperBowl? Of course, why not?
jokes.setup: Homer's confusion about holidays continues
jokes.punchline: Completely random 'Rabbit the SuperBowl' as if it's equivalent to other holidays
jokes.explanation: Random absurdism that feels less earned than the Father's Day setup

jokes.text: If I give you the money, what The gift comes from you? Where you get your money? That is 10. Make your own card.
jokes.setup: Child asking for money to buy a Father's Day gift
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical maternal logic about gift authenticity and budget
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Marge's practical parenting style

jokes.text: It is a ticket of 50. This kid finally refund. Very well, Ralph, look at what you can do with a 100 bill.
jokes.setup: Ralph trying to buy something with money
jokes.punchline: The amounts keep escalating ridiculously with Ralph's confusion
jokes.explanation: Good use of Ralph's characteristic confusion and escalating money amounts

jokes.text: You know, produced the saloperies mass do not what I feel for Dad. I'll give her a gift that comes from the heart.
jokes.setup: Lisa rejecting mass-produced gifts
jokes.punchline: Lisa's pretentious rejection of commercialism while everyone else is shopping
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - her intellectual superiority complex about gift-giving

jokes.text: Do not fear me, milady, Captain Superstud Dash is here with his small tool.
jokes.setup: Homer roleplaying with his Father's Day gift
jokes.punchline: Homer's ridiculous superhero persona with unintentional double entendre
jokes.explanation: Great Homer character comedy with his oblivious sexual innuendo and fantasy roleplay

jokes.text: Open another gift now would be like to go to a concert Jessica Simpson after going to the opera.
jokes.setup: Homer is so happy with Bart's gift
jokes.punchline: Homer's unexpectedly sophisticated cultural comparison
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion - Homer making a surprisingly cultured analogy is perfectly unexpected

jokes.text: A slight twinge in the heart? It is moving. We can not deny it.
jokes.setup: Lisa gives Homer a heartfelt handmade book
jokes.punchline: Homer's minimal emotional response to his daughter's sincere gift
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Homer's emotional limitations in a funny way

jokes.text: Well, it's not my fault. Magnet rotten!
jokes.setup: Lisa's book falls off the fridge, devastating her
jokes.punchline: Homer blaming the magnet instead of taking responsibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer deflection - refusing to take responsibility in the most Homer way possible

jokes.text: I brought you back even after losing our wedding album in a bet.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to reassure Marge he can win Lisa back
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing he bet away their wedding album
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy reveal showing Homer's past terrible decisions delivered casually

jokes.text: Hey, Moe, one might have to drink? Shut up and pass me to other heads of Moe.
jokes.setup: Someone asking Moe for a drink
jokes.punchline: Multiple Moe heads implying some bizarre situation
jokes.explanation: Weird visual gag that's typically Simpsons absurdist humor

jokes.text: A baby is 0 to 2 years. His first words. Play the game That is 50 cents less for babies. I prefer not to go rather than lying.
jokes.setup: Someone trying to get Lisa cheaper admission as a baby
jokes.punchline: Lisa's principled refusal to lie for 50 cents savings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - her moral integrity over trivial savings

jokes.text: Of course, is what all men do, take, take and take without attention to feelings and unicorns.
jokes.setup: Someone borrowing Lisa's crayon
jokes.punchline: Lisa's melodramatic feminist speech ending with 'and unicorns'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect Lisa character work - the unicorns addition is perfect absurdist touch

jokes.text: You do not think a girl can not blow a cable? Well, you're wrong!
jokes.setup: Assumption that a boy threw Lisa's backpack
jokes.punchline: Lisa's feminist outrage about cable-blowing capabilities
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Lisa's feminist anger extending to random technical abilities

jokes.text: Willie, rats were again mâchouillé cables. Leave me in peace. I am pasta ramen.
jokes.setup: Willie being told about rats chewing cables
jokes.punchline: Willie's dismissive response about making ramen pasta
jokes.explanation: Standard Willie character moment with his priorities

jokes.text: It can still keep drinks hot and cold? I'm afraid it keeps more no drink now.
jokes.setup: Lisa threw a thermos into the street during her rampage
jokes.punchline: Homer's concern about thermos functionality over Lisa's behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - caring more about thermos features than his daughter's behavioral problems

jokes.text: Ho, of course, it is easy to Blame me. It's harder to close.
jokes.setup: Psychologist blaming Homer for Lisa's issues
jokes.punchline: Homer's garbled attempt at 'it's harder to [something]' but trails off nonsensically
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer verbal fumbling when trying to defend himself

jokes.text: But how can I fix eight years crétinerie After two singles and not cost me anything?
jokes.setup: Homer needs to fix his relationship with Lisa
jokes.punchline: Homer's honest assessment of his parenting failures with budget constraints
jokes.explanation: Great self-aware Homer moment admitting to years of bad parenting while being cheap

jokes.text: What happened in the last Securi-Salamander? He is asleep in the costume and suffocated.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about the previous Safety Salamander
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of the previous mascot's death by suffocation
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy delivered matter-of-factly, very Simpsons style

jokes.text: The school bus never belts. So what protects you if we have an accident? A metal bar at the teeth.
jokes.setup: Homer teaching bus safety about seatbelts
jokes.punchline: Student's dark realistic observation about school bus 'safety'
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about real school bus safety issues delivered darkly

jokes.text: Are you a girl? Because I do not see any employees sausage
jokes.setup: Student asking Homer a question about gender
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual comment from a child to Homer in costume
jokes.explanation: Crude humor that feels cheap rather than clever

jokes.text: Every man for himself! Crush if you must!
jokes.setup: Homer's safety demonstration going wrong with fireworks
jokes.punchline: Homer abandoning his safety role and encouraging chaos
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer panic response - completely abandoning his safety role when things go wrong

jokes.text: Honey, you know that Dad is longer allowed to enter the zoo.
jokes.setup: Lisa wanting Homer to be like other fathers who take kids to zoo
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that Homer is banned from the zoo
jokes.explanation: Great casual reveal of Homer's past bad behavior, delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: I said, turn left at Boulevard Bart! I tell you that there is no Boulevard Bart!
jokes.setup: Homer giving impossible directions
jokes.punchline: Argument about non-existent street named after Bart
jokes.explanation: Good marital argument comedy showing Homer's impossible expectations and poor navigation.

jokes.text: Sort just your hunting knife and cut the cord 'ombrilical.' Yes Ma'am.
jokes.setup: Car accident scene with emergency
jokes.punchline: Requesting Homer cut an umbilical cord with a hunting knife in street emergency
jokes.explanation: Absurd emergency situation escalation typical of Simpsons chaos

jokes.text: Can we all agree not to involve our insurance? Come on, be nice.
jokes.setup: Major car accident scene
jokes.punchline: Driver's immediate concern about insurance rather than injuries
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about real-world priorities after accidents

jokes.text: Only three? Ok, six! It's better like that!
jokes.setup: Crowd gives three cheers for Homer
jokes.punchline: Mayor's arbitrary doubling of cheers to sound more generous
jokes.explanation: Good Quimby character moment showing his political grandstanding

jokes.text: Who the hell are you? Your responsible press. I knew I had to hire my nephew. I am your nephew. Ok, then it stinks.
jokes.setup: Reporter challenging Mayor Quimby
jokes.punchline: Quimby's nepotism revelation and immediate dismissal of his own hiring decision
jokes.explanation: Good corruption humor showing Quimby's casual nepotism and quick dismissal

jokes.text: Ask the plane anywhere, girls become truly wild.
jokes.setup: Explaining airplane hijacking attempt
jokes.punchline: Claiming it was about picking up women
jokes.explanation: Dark Quimby humor - treating serious crime as dating strategy fits his corrupt character.

jokes.text: I am proud to be American. I have done.
jokes.setup: Political candidates giving campaign speeches
jokes.punchline: Disco Stu's minimalist patriotic campaign platform
jokes.explanation: Perfect Disco Stu character moment - simple, direct, and appropriately disco-era patriotic

jokes.text: Are you sure there was no a miniature in his hat? I read an email about this.
jokes.setup: Lisa comparing Homer to Lincoln
jokes.punchline: Homer's conspiracy theory about Lincoln having a tiny person in his hat
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist Homer logic combining conspiracy theories with email misinformation

jokes.text: Well, I have a vision similar to X-rays, a breath of fire, and I can do laugh with tennis balls.
jokes.setup: Reporter asking about Homer's salamander powers as mayor
jokes.punchline: Homer's ridiculous list of made-up salamander abilities including tennis ball laughing
jokes.explanation: Good Homer absurdist biology mixed with the random tennis ball detail

jokes.text: And to think that during all these years I was a 'gecko man. Tire fool you.
jokes.setup: Learning about salamander powers
jokes.punchline: Reporter's revelation about being wrong about gecko abilities all these years
jokes.explanation: Follow-up absurdist moment about reptile confusion

jokes.text: Why should we let an election be influenced by a photo from several hours? Now, here candy restaurant for everyone.
jokes.setup: Photo of Homer strangling Bart raised as campaign issue
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissal of 'recent' evidence plus immediate distraction with candy
jokes.explanation: Good political deflection humor showing Homer's casual approach to scandal

jokes.text: People may not like Homer Simpson, but he loves this costume. In the same way they love this stupid American flag.
jokes.setup: Analysis of Homer's campaign success
jokes.punchline: Cynical comparison between costume worship and flag worship
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about symbolism over substance in politics

jokes.text: It's weird. At the beginning it was an egg.
jokes.setup: Homer looking at his costume
jokes.punchline: Homer's simple biological observation about salamanders
jokes.explanation: Simple Homer observation that's mildly amusing

jokes.text: Cats in the pants of everybody.
jokes.setup: Cat Lady answering policy question
jokes.punchline: Her solution to city problems is cats in everyone's pants
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cat Lady character moment - her one-track mind applied to governance

jokes.text: Cats in the pants of everybody.
jokes.setup: Cat Lady giving political answer about taxes
jokes.punchline: Her solution involves putting cats in everyone's pants
jokes.explanation: Perfect Crazy Cat Lady moment - completely insane policy proposal delivered with conviction.

jokes.text: Ok, dropped. I will not let a man tell me what to do. It is true, I am a man. a man dressed as a salamander for regain the love of his daughter.
jokes.setup: Homer's costume shrinking during debate
jokes.punchline: Homer's honest admission about being a man in costume for his daughter's love
jokes.explanation: Good moment of Homer honesty breaking through his political persona

jokes.text: Please stop giving fruit!
jokes.setup: Homer's campaign falling apart
jokes.punchline: Crowd member's specific complaint about fruit-throwing
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of crowd chaos with specific fruit complaint

jokes.text: I can not believe I lost with all the commercials that Ben Affleck has made. Yes, I t'avais warned about this.
jokes.setup: Homer's election loss
jokes.punchline: Homer blaming loss on Ben Affleck commercials (presumably bad ones)
jokes.explanation: Decent cultural reference humor about celebrity endorsement backfiring

jokes.text: you no longer tail now. I know... I sense yet.
jokes.setup: Homer out of salamander costume
jokes.punchline: Phantom limb sensation for costume tail
jokes.explanation: Clever physical comedy concept about costume attachment, good character integration.
